title: citharexylum fruticosum f. subserratum
intro: Citharexylum fruticosum f. subserratum, also known as the Mexican honeysuckle, is a flowering plant species in the Verbenaceae family, native to Mexico and parts of Central America. The plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine for its medicinal properties, including anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties. The health benefits of Citharexylum fruticosum f. subserratum include its potential to treat various health issues such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, including teas and infusions, have been used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
